data.title: Alibaba (BABA) Stock Is Rising Again — Is an AI Chip IPO the Next Catalyst?
data.description:
Alibaba (BABA) is moving closer to a potential public listing of its AI chip unit, T-Head, according to a Bloomberg report, as investor interest grows in AI chipmakers amid limits on Nvidia's (NVDA) products in China. Following the report, Alibaba's U.S.-listed shares rose about 5% in pre-market trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Bloomberg said Alibaba plans to restructure T-Head into a separate business with partial employee ownership, a step often taken ahead of a listing. The company is exploring an IPO, though no timeline has been set.

Founded in 2018, T-Head designs chips for computing and storage. While it remains smaller than global leaders, it has made steady progress at home. The unit recently signed a deal to supply AI accelerators for a large data center run by China Unicom, placing its chips alongside other domestic rivals.

Why China's AI Chip Push Is Gaining Speed

AI chips are in short supply, and demand is rising fast as companies build larger data centers and AI systems. In China, this has increased the push to cut reliance on U.S. chipmakers, lifting interest in local chip developers like T-Head.

Recent listings and IPO plans by other Chinese chip firms have also drawn strong investor interest. Expectations of policy support from Beijing are adding to that momentum, even though many of these companies are still in early stages.

Beyond Alibaba, other Chinese tech giants, including Baidu (BIDU), are also developing their own chips as they look for local alternatives to Nvidia hardware, which faces export limits into China.

AI Sits at the Center of Alibaba's Strategy

Alibaba has been designing its own chips for years to support its cloud and data center business. As it pushes deeper into AI, that chip work is becoming more important, linking software, services, and the hardware needed to run them.

Alibaba is backing this strategy with heavy spending. Chief executive Eddie Wu has pledged more than $53 billion for AI and infrastructure, underlining how important this shift is to Alibaba's future.

Is Alibaba Stock a Buy Now?   

Wall Street remains constructive on the stock. Alibaba stock carries a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 14 unanimous Buy ratings over the past three months. The average BABA price target stands at $203.09, suggesting roughly 20.41% upside from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 1/22/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Climb after Indexes Rebound, Inflation Data in Sight
data.description:
U.S. stock futures rose early Thursday after the three indexes marked a major rebound in the previous session, aided by President Trump's easing tariff stance. Trump announced at the World Economic Forum that he would not proceed with his planned tariffs on February 1 and mentioned that no force would be used to acquire Greenland.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.77%, 0.52%, and 0.29%, respectively, at 3:59 a.m. EST on January 22.

During Wednesday's regular trading session, the three major averages closed higher. The Dow added 1.21%, while the S&P 500 gained 1.16%, and the Nasdaq Composite climbed 1.18%. Trump announced he would halt new tariffs on Europe scheduled for February 1. He also revealed a "framework" agreement on Greenland, easing global investor worries over a forced acquisition.

Turning toward the economic calendar, investors now focus on Thursday's release of the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) price index, the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge. Data on weekly initial jobless claims will also be released today.

On the earnings front, companies reporting today include Intel (INTC), Visa (V), and GE Aerospace (GE).

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down, floating near 4.23%. WTI crude oil futures were trending lower, hovering near $60.49 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price decreased to nearly $4,827 per ounce on Thursday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened higher today following U.S. President Trump's announcement of a "framework" deal for Greenland and his decision to walk back planned tariffs on Europe.

Asia-Pacific Markets Trade Higher

Asia-Pacific markets also rebounded on January 22 amid a cooling geopolitical environment.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index gained 0.17%. In China, the Shanghai Composite rose 0.14%, while the Shenzhen Component added 0.67%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei advanced 1.73%, and the Topix surged 0.74%.

data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Quantum Computing News: D-Wave (QBTS) Is No Longer a One-Trick Quantum Stock
data.description:
Welcome to Thursday's update on quantum computing. As the market moves deeper into 2026, the focus continues to shift from lab work toward funding flows, public market strategy, and real business planning. As always, this update highlights clear developments that matter for investors watching the quantum space.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Let's begin.

China Funding Signals Commercial Push

First, SpinQ Technology completed a Series C funding round worth hundreds of millions of yuan. At current rates, that range is about $14 million to $40 million or more. The company said the funds will support core technology work, deeper industry ties, and faster global reach. Founder Xiang Jinggen said the capital will help drive industrial growth and expand real use cases. The round shows that China-based quantum firms are still attracting strong backing as they move from research toward delivery.

D-Wave Expands into Gate Model Systems

Next, D-Wave Quantum (QBTS) completed its acquisition of Quantum Circuits Inc. This move adds gate model quantum systems to D-Wave's existing annealing platform. Chief Executive Alan Baratz said the deal helps address a wider range of customer problems. Quantum Circuits brings error-corrected superconducting qubits that aim to speed up. As a result, D-Wave is now the only public company offering two quantum approaches under one roof.

IBM Flags Enterprise Readiness Gap

Meanwhile, International Business Machines Corporation (IBM) released new research on enterprise planning. The firm found that 59% of executives expect quantum-enabled AI to reshape their industry by 2030. However, only 27% expect their firms to use quantum systems by then. IBM called this a planning gap rather than a timing issue. The study argues that quantum will enter workflows slowly as a support tool for AI and classical systems.

Energy Limits Bring Hardware Choices Into Focus

At the same time, the World Economic Forum discussed how energy use is becoming a key factor in computing strategy. Classical systems face physical limits tied to heat and power. Quantum systems may reduce energy use for certain tasks if hardware overhead stays low. Superconducting systems often draw about 25 kilowatts due to cooling needs. In contrast, neutral-atom systems can run below 10 kilowatts. These gaps matter as data center power demand rises toward 2030.

Error Correction Breakthrough Cuts Hardware Needs

Finally, Alice and Bob, a French-based quantum firm, reported progress in quantum error correction using new Elevator Codes. The firm said the method cut logical error rates by 10,000 times while requiring only about three times as many qubits. The design builds on cat qubits that already resist some errors by design. Researcher Diego Ruiz said the advance could allow complex simulation work sooner than expected. This result highlights how software design can ease pressure on hardware scale.

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to line up all the stocks appearing in the piece alongside quantum computing stocks. It's a quick way to see how they stack up and where the field could be heading.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Is ON Semiconductor Stock (ON) a Good Buy in 2026?
data.description:
ON Semiconductor's (ON) prospects in 2026 look stable. The company is expected to benefit from the continued expansion of the global chip market, driven by AI, automotive, energy, and industrial demand. However, a potential oversupply of power semiconductors could pressure pricing and margins for companies even as long‑term demand remains strong. Currently, ON stock is a Buy, according to the technical indicators, implying further upside from current levels.

data.title: AMD Stock Soars — Is It Still a Buy Ahead of Q4 Earnings?
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock has been on a strong run, hitting a year-to-date high and drawing investors' attention. So far in 2026, AMD has gained nearly 17%, following a roughly 70% surge last year. Looking ahead, analysts remain bullish ahead of its Q4 earnings scheduled for February 3. AMD's average price target of $284.93 suggests about 14% upside from current levels, with the highest target of $377 implying more than 50% potential gains.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Yesterday, AMD stock rose 8%, and it was up another 1% in pre-market trading today. The recent surge came after the company appointed KC McClure to its board, bringing in her strong background in finance.

AMD's Q4 Earnings Expectations

For Q4 2025, analysts expect AMD to report earnings of $1.32 per share, up from $1.09 in the same quarter last year. Revenue is projected at $9.63 billion, compared with $7.65 billion in Q4 2024.

On the plus side, Big Tech's heavy investment in AI chips, along with expanding data centers, has increased demand for server CPUs—a market where AMD has been steadily gaining share from Intel (INTC). Analysts expect this trend to boost sales of AMD's traditional data center chips, which are often used alongside AI GPUs in servers.

Notably, in AMD's key data center segment, which includes its AI chips, revenue rose 22% to $4.3 billion in the previous quarter. Below is a screenshot of AMD's revenue breakdown across segments.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Goldman Sachs (GS) Director Dumps $65M in Stock. Should Investors Worry?
data.description:
Goldman Sachs' (GS) Director David Viniar sold shares of the investment bank in a series of transactions, totaling over $65 million, on January 16. It is worth noting that large insider sales often raise eyebrows, but they do not always mean something is wrong.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

According to the SEC filing, the sale included a large block of 64,796 shares, which generated nearly $62.5 million in proceeds, plus another smaller sale of 3,390 shares worth over $3 million.

A Big Sale After a Big Rally

The timing is notable. Goldman stock has climbed more than 36% over the past six months, fueled by strong earnings and rising optimism around dealmaking and capital markets. With the stock at multi-year highs, insiders may see this as an ideal moment to lock in gains.

Despite this large sale, the director still owns GS stock worth about $162.91 million. This suggests he is not walking away from the company, just trimming exposure.

Goldman's Fundamentals Remain Solid

Goldman's fundamentals look much stronger than they did a year ago. The company has been lifted by a rebound in IPOs and M&A activity, solid performance in both fixed income and equities trading, and cost-cutting efforts that have boosted profitability.

It has also been shifting focus toward steadier revenue streams such as wealth management. Together, these trends have helped lift the stock and restore investor confidence.

Insider Sentiment for GS Stock Is Bearish

Overall, TipRanks' Insider Trading Activity Tool shows that insider confidence in GS stock is currently Negative.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.description:
American refining rivals Valero Energy (VLO) and Phillips 66 (PSX) have emerged among the first to snap up a discounted purchase of Venezuela's crude oil, Reuters reported on Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.

data.title: Compal Electronics Warns AI-Driven Memory Costs May Pressure PC Margins Through 2027
data.description:
Taiwan-based Compal Electronics warned that rising memory prices will continue to weigh on the tech industry through 2027. The company is one of the world's largest makers of notebooks and personal computers for major global brands.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At a press event in Taipei, Compal said global PC and notebook shipments are likely to fall by a low single-digit percentage in 2026. Even so, the company expects its own PC business to stay flat or see mild growth due to its customer mix.

Chief Executive Officer Anthony Peter Bonadero said the pressure comes from memory costs that are rising faster than expected. "It is a true super cycle that we have not really seen," Bonadero said, referring to memory chips.

AI Demand Is Reshaping the Cost Structure

The warning adds fresh detail to a trend already visible across the market, with reports that memory prices have jumped about 50% as AI data centers absorb supply.

According to Compal, memory used to make up about 15% to 18% of a PC's material cost. That share can now reach 35% to 40%, which leaves far less room for profit.

At the same time, the world's three largest memory chip makers are focused on AI servers. Samsung Electronics (SSNLF), SK Hynix, and Micron Technology (MU) have all said demand tied to AI remains very strong.

Bonadero said these suppliers are giving priority to high-bandwidth memory used in AI systems. As a result, less supply is available for everyday devices like PCs and laptops.

What It Means for Investors

As memory costs stay high, PC makers face limited options. They can accept lower margins or pass higher costs on to buyers. Meanwhile, memory suppliers continue to benefit from firm pricing and strong demand tied to AI spending. This supports the broader view that AI growth is changing not only data centers, but also the cost base of consumer hardware.

Taken together, Compal's comments reinforce a simple point. AI-driven memory demand is no longer a short-term issue, and its impact is likely to last well beyond 2026.

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to align all stocks mentioned in the text with other smartphone makers and chip manufacturers, which are also affected by the current landscape.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Memory Prices Jump 50% as AI Data Centers Tighten Chip Supply
data.description:
Global demand for phones, personal computers, and game consoles is expected to fall this year as rising memory chip costs force companies to raise prices. The main driver of the memory shortage is heavy spending by major U.S. tech firms on AI data centers. As a result, a large share of the world's memory chip supply is being pulled away from consumer devices.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Companies such as OpenAI, Alphabet (GOOGL), and Microsoft (MSFT) are buying huge volumes of memory for AI servers. This has pushed chip makers to focus on higher-margin data center sales. In turn, prices for memory used in everyday electronics have surged.

Samsung Electronics (SSNLF), SK Hynix, and Micron Technology (MU), the three largest memory chip producers, have all reported strong earnings as prices climbed. However, those higher costs are now spreading across consumer markets. Research firms IDC and Counterpoint now expect global smartphone sales to fall by at least 2% this year. That would be the first yearly decline since 2023.

At the same time, IDC expects the global PC market to shrink by about 4.9% in 2026 after solid growth last year. Console sales are also expected to drop by more than 4%, according to TrendForce.

Consumer Devices Face Pricing Pressure

As memory costs rise, hardware makers face difficult choices. They can absorb the higher costs and accept lower margins, or they can raise prices and risk weaker demand. Many firms are already passing some of those costs to buyers.

"Manufacturers might absorb some costs, but given the scale of the shortage, it is certainly going to show up as higher prices for consumers," said Jacob Bourne, an analyst at Emarketer. He added that this trend is likely to weigh on device sales through 2026.

Counterpoint estimates memory prices could rise another 40% to 50% in the first quarter, following a 50% jump last year. Tobey Gonnerman, president of Fusion Worldwide, said, "Over the last two quarters, we've seen 1,000% price inflation in some products and pricing is continuing to rise."

The impact is expected to hit low and mid-range device makers the hardest. These include Xiaomi (XIACF), Lenovo (LNVGY), and TCL Technology, which operate with thinner margins. TrendForce said Dell Technologies (DELL)  and Lenovo were planning price increases of up to 20% early in 2026

Meanwhile, Apple (AAPL) is seen as better positioned than most rivals. The company has strong pricing power and long-term supply contracts that limit exposure to sudden price swings. Apple also tends to keep U.S. iPhone prices steady between product launches.

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to align all stocks mentioned in the text with other smartphone makers and chip manufacturers, which are also affected by the current landscape.
data.author: Ran Melamed
